120174.explanation: NBB would like to see a reflection of the Noord-Beveland population in council and college: young, old, men and women. But for the college, the right person for the right portfolio(s) is key.

120175.explanation: A consultative or binding referendum? NBB stands for careful participation; all stakeholders should be heard on important issues, whether informing, consulting, or advising. Council members are elected representatives charged with making the right balance of interests.

120178.explanation: According to NBB, there are currently (sufficient) possibilities within existing laws and regulations. It must be a secondary activity linked to an active agricultural business.

120179.explanation: NBB supports sustainability, cooperation, and innovative solutions. Naturally, a municipality should support such initiatives, administratively and possibly with subsidies.

120180.explanation: NBB wants to support local entrepreneurs. Offering a good business climate for all entrepreneurs, including those from outside. But given the scale of NB and existing infrastructure, a large distribution center is not desirable.

120182.explanation: The same applies to temporary employees at holiday parks and in retail.

120186.explanation: NBB is open to thinking along and possibly facilitating such an initiative if the request comes from an initiator, but would not proactively pursue this as a municipality itself.

120187.explanation: NBB is happy with the recycling center as it is now arranged and finds free collection is not a sustainable solution (fuel costs and personnel).

120190.explanation: NBB wants to stimulate housing development but does not see building houses on farmers’ land as a solution. Why only with farmers? This really calls for tailor-made solutions. NBB believes affordability must always be guaranteed.

120197.explanation: NBB believes everyone must feel safe on Noord-Beveland and where enforcement is needed, it must be fully deployed.

120198.explanation: NBB believes not everything needs to be documented, partly for the privacy of our residents.

120203.explanation: Recreational homes are largely unsuitable for permanent residence, as is the infrastructure in the parks. NBB would consider allowing a small percentage of permanent residence for supervision and safety reasons.

120204.explanation: NBB supports adding a neighborhood or small street, but only if it fits urbanistically and landscape-wise and is proportionate in size to the current core.

120205.explanation: NBB would like to see everyone participate, no matter how small, in this society. So stimulate participation but do not impose it as a requirement.

120206.explanation: NBB stands for generous social care policy!

120207.explanation: This has already been taken into account by increasing to 130% for the declaration fund, and NBB fully supports this.

120209.explanation: NBB believes every car should be able to park.

120210.explanation: NBB stands for safe roads and villages.

120211.explanation: NBB wants every resident to be able to access the town hall, in any language. The best care should be available to everyone, but it does not necessarily have to match faith, culture, or beliefs.

120215.explanation: This tax must be balanced with our tourism product but also be used for our infrastructure and tourism policy.

120216.explanation: As long as there is demand and land available, according to NBB this is no problem, but only for local entrepreneurs or businesses appropriate to the scale of Noord-Beveland.

120218.explanation: NBB believes that as long as this is legislation, we must comply. But NBB wants more flexible housing and new social rental housing where local residents get priority.

120223.explanation: It is important to pass on history to the youth visually and in language, according to NBB.

120226.explanation: NBB believes houses in the core are for permanent residence. Individual room rentals can be a solution for housing shortages among young people.

120227.explanation: NBB believes that extra attention should be paid to this in new housing projects but also welcomes solutions for the busy streets in Colijnsplaat.

120228.explanation: NBB believes our island offers enough outdoor space to walk dogs.

120229.explanation: NBB does not want (recreational) homes on the coast or waterfront and is happy with the coast vision and Veerse Meer vision that protect our coast. But for NBB, dilapidated harbor areas are an exception. Permanent housing could add value for the respective villages.

120230.explanation: NBB supports protecting our nature reserves and, where necessary, temporary closures but with clear explanations to our residents and visitors.

120231.explanation: NBB finds a maximum of 21 meters acceptable.

120174.explanation: The VVD wants more women in leadership but opposes a mandatory quota as stated in the proposition. Quality and suitability must always prevail. The party prefers to encourage female talent intake through scouting and the Liberal Women’s Network.

120175.explanation: The VVD is against referendums. We believe in the power of the municipal council to carefully weigh all interests. Instead of a simple yes or no afterward, we prefer residents to genuinely participate from the start. This ensures supported solutions that benefit everyone.

120178.explanation: The VVD supports farmers who want to start a shop or mini-campsite as long as they actively work as farmers. This strengthens income and the tourism vision. We want to give entrepreneurs space as long as it fits within an active agricultural business and the landscape. Entrepreneurship is the engine of our island.

120179.explanation: The VVD supports sustainable initiatives but opposes structural municipal subsidies. We want sustainability to remain feasible and affordable without tax increases. The municipality should facilitate and remove barriers, but residents must keep their own financial responsibility.

120180.explanation: The VVD opposes mega halls and the overdevelopment ('verdozing') of our island. We choose economic growth that fits a human scale. New businesses are welcome, provided they are small-scale, create local jobs, and do not harm the unique peace and open space of Noord-Beveland. Quality over quantity.

120182.explanation: The VVD supports housing on own terrain to keep the agricultural sector flexible. Entrepreneurs must have the space to arrange this themselves, provided quality and safety are assured. Fast procedures are essential. Collaboration with certified partners can also be an option.

120186.explanation: The VVD is against a mandatory fireworks show. Municipalities must make their own choices based on local needs. We want to keep New Year celebrations festive without unnecessary bureaucracy or high costs for residents. Tailoring and safety are paramount. A show for six villages is not feasible.

120187.explanation: The VVD supports free bulky waste collection at home. This prevents illegal dumping and keeps our neighborhoods and countryside clean. We want good service for residents without extra costs per pickup, financed from existing waste collection levies.

120190.explanation: The VVD wants farmers to be able to build housing more easily on their own land. This helps combat the housing shortage and provides farmers with an additional income model. We advocate for faster procedures and fewer rules for splitting farms or building an extra dwelling in the countryside.

120192.explanation: The VVD opposes additional subsidies for the local broadcaster. We believe the broadcaster must operate more independently. Through regional cooperation, quality can improve without extra taxpayer money from our residents. This is working well in our province with Streekomroep and Omroep Zeeland.

120197.explanation: The VVD favors more enforcement officers. Due to minimal police deployment, we have advocated for an extra neighborhood enforcement officer to increase safety and visibility in the centers. More enforcement officers ensure rapid response to nuisance and strengthen the sense of security on our island.

120198.explanation: The VVD supports targeted camera surveillance at high-risk locations such as harbors and business parks. It helps combat crime and undermining where physical enforcement is limited. Safety is paramount, provided residents' privacy is guaranteed through smart and local use.

120203.explanation: The VVD opposes permanent residence in holiday homes. Recreation parks must retain their tourist function for our local economy. Strict enforcement prevents deterioration and misuse; for permanent living, we prefer to build structural homes in our six village centers.

120204.explanation: The VVD opposes a ban on building outside village boundaries. To keep young people and families on our island, new construction on village edges is necessary. We choose vital villages with sufficient housing and the preservation of local amenities by enabling controlled expansion.

120205.explanation: The VVD believes that benefits should not be taken lightly. A social contribution proportional to one’s ability is good for society and helps people return to work faster. We choose reciprocity: giving something back for the safety net the community provides.

120206.explanation: The VVD wants debt forgiveness only for those who truly cannot pay. We oppose an automatic stop but want stricter controls on assets. Working must always yield more than a benefit; we prevent abuse through stringent necessity checks.

120207.explanation: This has already been increased to 130%. The VVD opposes raising the income threshold. Working must always yield more than a benefit. We want to reduce burdens for everyone and focus on tailored support via the social desk for those who truly need help, instead of new general schemes.

120209.explanation: The VVD supports more charging points as long as they are demand-driven and installed by the market. We do not want unnecessary loss of public parking spaces in the centers. The municipality should facilitate, but entrepreneurs should operate them so that costs do not fall on taxpayers.

120210.explanation: This is already the case in our centers. Safety through design: 30 km/h is only useful in specific residential streets or near schools, provided the road layout supports it; placing a sign alone does not work according to the VVD. The VVD opposes lowering speed limits as symbolic politics against cars.

120211.explanation: The VVD supports freedom of choice in healthcare. Patients should have control over their own care process, provided quality and affordability are maintained. We believe personal preferences should be respected as long as they fit within available care options.

120215.explanation: Tourist tax will be kept at an acceptable level in the coming years. By having tourists fairly contribute to our facilities, we maintain the balance between living and recreation. In line with our tourism vision, this ensures the burden on our residents remains low.

120216.explanation: Where possible, we create space for businesses to establish themselves well. The RIP Kamperland is a good example. Many plots have been allocated to local entrepreneurs, which we encourage.

120218.explanation: The VVD opposes preferential treatment for status holders in the housing market. Local house seekers must not be disadvantaged. We want everyone to get a fair turn and advocate for temporary reception locations to reduce pressure on social rental housing.

120223.explanation: The VVD values Zeeuws as a valuable heritage, but the content of lessons is up to the schools themselves. We oppose municipal interference in the curriculum. The focus should be on basic skills such as Dutch and math; attention to dialect is a free choice of schools and parents.

120226.explanation: The VVD supports room rental to quickly address the housing shortage. By better utilizing existing homes, we create space for young people and urgent seekers. We want fewer rules for landlords, provided neighborhood livability is maintained through good agreements and enforcement.

120227.explanation: The VVD wants sufficient parking spaces in the centers. Good accessibility is essential for our local entrepreneurs and residents’ convenience. We choose practical solutions that reduce parking pressure in residential areas and strengthen the vitality of our villages.

120228.explanation: The VVD opposes mandatory dog toilets in every village center. We rely on the responsibility of dog owners and do not want unnecessary costs that increase local taxes. We prefer to focus on enforcing the cleaning-up obligation to keep our villages clean.

120229.explanation: The VVD opposes a ban on living by the water. Living by the water is a strength of our island. We want to enable quality new construction provided it is landscape-friendly and ensures water safety. This keeps Noord-Beveland attractive and makes use of the opportunities of our unique location.

120230.explanation: The VVD is against closing off nature areas. Nature is meant to be enjoyed. We want our island to remain accessible for residents and tourists. Recreation and nature can coexist well through smart zoning instead of prohibitions that limit freedom of movement.

120231.explanation: The VVD supports small-scale wind turbines on farms for own energy consumption. This helps farmers become more sustainable and reduce costs. We want faster procedures for these farm turbines but remain opposed to large-scale wind parks that affect our open landscape.

120174.explanation: We prefer to look at quality and experience. A good mix can be just fine.

120175.explanation: The SGP believes that citizens choose politicians every four years to make balanced decisions on their behalf. Councillors do have the duty and responsibility to remain in touch with what lives in society. Complex issues do not always fit into a yes/no question.

120178.explanation: There must be clear and consistent policy on what a farm enterprise may or may not do. New campsites are not possible on Noord-Beveland; mini-campsites are possible under conditions.

120179.explanation: There is a generous subsidy policy for sustainability and we continue to look for improvements that align with the impact on generating sustainable energy.

120180.explanation: Noord-Beveland is not the place for new distribution centers.

120182.explanation: Under good conditions, the municipality can facilitate this well. We advocate a clear policy on this.

120186.explanation: Could be an alternative to the fireworks ban from 2026.

120187.explanation: We are very happy with our recycling center! Everyone who lives here permanently can use it for free.

120190.explanation: Designating where building is allowed in the municipality is a municipal task. For that we have a municipal vision that needs to be updated.

120192.explanation: What is local broadcasting? And who will pay and who will not? Moreover, objectivity is very decisive.

120197.explanation: Always a balance between violations, behavior, and deployment of enforcement officers. We believe increased police deployment should have higher priority.

120198.explanation: On industrial estates in any case. And if there is a necessity, also. At the same time, we must not unnecessarily infringe on people's privacy.

120203.explanation: We disagree with this. For us, those are truly separate accommodations. Holiday parks are not built for permanent residence. We must be careful not to use the current market situation to do things we will regret, as happened in the past.

120204.explanation: In principle, first build as much as possible within the municipal boundaries. But new homes adjacent to the village must certainly be possible.

120205.explanation: Always keep looking for possibilities for this!

120206.explanation: We can help people who are really struggling financially with this.

120207.explanation: Poverty is and remains distressing for those affected. We are happy to help those who need it.

120209.explanation: Often comes at the expense of regular parking spaces, which we always seem to lack.

120215.explanation: Our tourists may enjoy our beautiful island. A responsible increase of this tax is our starting point to financially relieve our residents more.

120216.explanation: We want to gladly facilitate (our) entrepreneurs with space offerings for those who need it. They are very important for the livability of the villages.

120218.explanation: For us, it goes too far that our own residents have less chance of getting a home than others. Unless there is a compelling necessity.

120226.explanation: That could lead to reduced well-being in the environment due to nuisance and parking problems.

120228.explanation: Provided there is demonstrable need by our own residents with responsibility for maintenance etc. of the users.

120230.explanation: Let everyone enjoy our beautiful nature reserves! We often see that they are still visited too little.

120231.explanation: Always keep looking for responsible and necessary generation. Smaller wind turbines can be an option near or on farms.

120174.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in our municipality. With an equal number of men and women, that is. In such a government, all perspectives are represented to make fair decisions.

120175.explanation: Residents should never feel they are lagging behind or that decisions are made in an opaque way. Residents often know best what their village needs. For many subjects, a referendum can be a fair and good way to make a decision.

120178.explanation: We support the development of farm shops. For mini-campings, we set a ceiling and these may only support an existing farm business, not as a replacement.

120179.explanation: The ideal picture: residents jointly generate their own green and affordable electricity with a wind turbine or solar panels. They use the electricity on the same spot, so the grid is not burdened. Energy cooperatives are an asset to our municipality, and we must encourage them.

120180.explanation: In recent years, far too many huge distribution centers have been built in the Netherlands. They spoil the landscape and much of the work is done by low-paid migrant workers. Moreover, the location of Noord-Beveland is unsuitable for much truck traffic.

120182.explanation: Those who bring people to our municipality to work must provide decent housing themselves. Without our residents suffering from it. Therefore, we also ensure good control and points of contact. There is no place for exploitation and nuisance in our municipality.

120186.explanation: The best New Year's party is celebrated together and fireworks are not necessary for that. If the municipality organizes something, we do so without fireworks, for example with light shows and sound. To honor traditions, we will continue to allow the annual carbidschieten (carbide shooting).

120187.explanation: By not collecting at home, we can maintain our own environmental station.

120190.explanation: A farmer is not a project developer and the countryside has different conditions than the villages, making it less suitable for many homes. A caregiver's house on their own property should be possible.

120192.explanation: Local media are extremely important to know what is happening in your own area, from the street to the municipal council. But many local media struggle to keep afloat. We believe the municipality should contribute to this.

120197.explanation: Everyone must feel free and safe. We tackle crime and ensure our villages are inclusive and pleasant to live in. We focus on prevention and cooperation between schools, caregivers, and residents so problems remain small and our villages stay strong and livable.

120198.explanation: Camera surveillance does not solve the problems, enforcement and prevention do. Only if nothing else works can camera surveillance be a tool.

120203.explanation: Holiday parks are meant for vacationing, not living. And they are not suitable for that. We do not want villages next to villages, we prefer natural growth of our existing villages and facilities. We must build for residents, not for tourists.

120204.explanation: We want to preserve and protect the vastness of our landscape as best as possible. But to tackle the housing shortage, we must also use agricultural land for housing. That is also an opportunity to create pleasant, green neighborhoods for our residents.

120205.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

120206.explanation: If the waiving of municipal taxes stops, people with the lowest incomes in our municipality will be in big trouble. They simply cannot miss that money. We believe everyone should be able to keep their head above water, so we are in favor of waivers.

120207.explanation: It is important that available resources are targeted at those who need them most. The income limit for poverty assistance in our municipality is already higher than the national average. This also reaches residents who are just above the minimum.

120209.explanation: If there is demonstrable demand for more charging points for electric cars, the municipality must certainly cooperate. Extra charging points must match the demand and may not unnecessarily reduce the available parking space.

120210.explanation: With a lower maximum speed, traffic in our municipality becomes safer. For cycling children and elderly people with mobility issues, this makes a world of difference. Moreover, the street becomes quieter and the air cleaner.

120211.explanation: People must be able to choose care that fits their faith, culture, or belief. We do acknowledge that in practice this is not always possible due to limited availability in the region, but the principle of freedom of choice must remain leading.

120215.explanation: We are in favor of increasing the tourist tax; the rate on Noord-Beveland is currently very low. By taxing tourists more, we can keep taxes low for our residents.

120216.explanation: There is currently sufficient space available on the industrial estates; expansion is not necessary. Further expansion of industrial estates will be coordinated with neighboring municipalities, and there is still enough space available in the region.

120218.explanation: In Noord-Beveland, the waiting list for social rental homes is relatively short compared to other parts of the country. Therefore, it is not necessary to give status holders extra priority at the expense of other groups; a fair distribution of homes is well possible here.

120223.explanation: We believe that primary schools should have the freedom to give attention to the Zeelandic dialect. Preserving the regional language contributes to culture, identity, and pride among children, and schools may determine themselves how they incorporate this in their educational program.

120226.explanation: There is a great shortage of homes and many people are looking for an affordable place. Renting out rooms often happens in poor condition and is relatively expensive, so we believe this is not a solution for the housing shortage in our municipality without additional (quality) conditions.

120227.explanation: Bottlenecks in the villages must be addressed, especially in unsafe situations regarding access for emergency services. There are enough places in the villages, unclear signage for day-trippers and other visitors does cause problems. That deserves more attention than extra places.

120228.explanation: A dog walking area reduces nuisance in inappropriate places, helps dogs burn off energy, and promotes their socialization. Moreover, it is a meeting place for residents and contributes to the health and well-being of both dogs and owners.

120229.explanation: We believe that no new homes should be built on the coast or waterfront. These areas are vulnerable due to climate change, rising sea levels, and increasing risks of water nuisance. For developments outside the dikes, water safety and natural values must be paramount.

120230.explanation: Nature and landscape should remain accessible to residents and visitors. It is therefore undesirable to permanently close off natural areas to the public. Only when necessary, such as to protect breeding birds or vulnerable nature, are targeted or temporary measures appropriate.

120231.explanation: The current regulations for wind turbines on farms are adequate. Installing a wind turbine is already possible under certain conditions. We believe the current policy aligns well with support, landscape, and multiple land uses.

120174.explanation: Ultimately, what matters is that the parties with the most votes provide an alderman with the right skills. It could therefore also be the case that the council has more women than men.

120175.explanation: It is always good to conduct a poll among the population on truly important issues. This can be done via Noord-Beveland denkt mee or residents’ meetings, for example. A referendum is essentially the same; we like to hear from our residents about what matters.

120178.explanation: As CDA, we support giving an active farm business an extra economic boost by adding, for example, a shop or mini-campsite.

120179.explanation: As CDA, we support residents taking their own initiative, such as the postcoderoos initiative, where solar panels can be issued through a cooperative on someone else’s roof.

120180.explanation: A large-scale distribution center does not fit on Noord-Beveland. We want space for our own (smaller) entrepreneurs on Noord-Beveland’s industrial estates.

120182.explanation: We support that migrant workers are housed on the entrepreneur(s)’ own property. This way, control lies with the entrepreneur and migrant workers do not occupy homes in the centers where there is currently a shortage.

120186.explanation: If fireworks are banned, that is definitely a good idea.

120187.explanation: We support free collection of bulky waste because some residents do not have the opportunity to bring it themselves to the recycling center.

120190.explanation: This would create an undesirable situation because if one farmer is allowed, then everyone is. The municipality must have control via the environmental plan.

120192.explanation: We support this and it is already happening.

120197.explanation: It would be great if extra capacity becomes available for Noord-Beveland.

120198.explanation: In places where there is frequent nuisance (from youths), this could contribute to a safer environment.

120203.explanation: Noord-Beveland has a well-functioning policy with designated areas for permanent and recreational residence. Allowing this everywhere would cause nuisance between different user groups.

120204.explanation: Some centers on Noord-Beveland can certainly have a new neighborhood built, as is currently happening in Colijnsplaat.

120205.explanation: This also gives someone on social assistance extra purpose in life and gets them among people, which reduces their distance from society.

120206.explanation: Residents with a low income cannot always do anything about it themselves; it is good that the municipality offers extra support where possible and allowed.

120207.explanation: There is increasing hidden poverty among people who actually work. Often, these residents are just on the edge. A slight relaxation could offer a solution here.

120209.explanation: There are more and more rechargeable cars; if more charging stations are installed where there is a shortage, everyone can charge their car near their home.

120210.explanation: 30 kilometers per hour is fine for our villages.

120211.explanation: It is about the quality of care and the caregiver. Faith, culture, and beliefs are of secondary importance.

120215.explanation: As Noord-Beveland, we have a unique tourism experience product; we can market it as such. The aim is to have the lowest tourist tax among the coastal municipalities.

120216.explanation: There is high demand for industrial estate space on Noord-Beveland from our own entrepreneurs. A suitable expansion would definitely be appropriate.

120218.explanation: Status holders have, for good reasons, priority on some social rental homes. The municipality also has a role here, but this certainly does not apply to every rental property.

120223.explanation: We naturally think Zeelandic is a beautiful language, but it does not belong in primary schools. Schools already struggle to cover the mandatory curriculum in the school year.

120226.explanation: Bed & breakfast is already possible, which is basically the same. What is important is that this is properly reported and that tourist tax is paid.

120227.explanation: It would be great to create larger parking lots on the edges of the centers. With good signage, parking pressure in the centers themselves can be reduced.

120228.explanation: There is demand from every center for a dog walking area; it is therefore good that the municipality strives to find and realize these.

120229.explanation: We must be careful with our coast and therefore no longer allow new homes here.

120230.explanation: Besides various closed nature areas, there are strips closed to the public during the breeding season; this is fine. An expansion seems unnecessary.

120231.explanation: The current regulation allowing a small wind turbine on the farm is sufficient; more is not needed according to the CDA.

120174.explanation: Aldermen should be chosen based on their capabilities, not their gender.

120175.explanation: The current council talked a lot about citizen participation but did very little. WzNB wants the municipality to listen to citizens and is open to direct democracy through a referendum.

120178.explanation: WzNB wants to support farmers and local entrepreneurs, but not everything can be allowed everywhere. Tailored solutions are needed and maximum numbers may apply.

120179.explanation: It is important for residents to take joint steps toward sustainable energy. The energy cooperative Energiek Noord-Beveland deserves more support.

120180.explanation: Distribution centers are often huge metal boxes attracting a lot of traffic and dominating the landscape. Noord-Beveland is not a suitable place for that.

120182.explanation: We cannot do without seasonal workers to harvest fruits, especially in horticulture and fruit growing. Seasonal workers perform work for which too few people are available. Employers must provide housing, but this can also be elsewhere.

120186.explanation: Fireworks are fun but should be limited. A fireworks show on New Year’s Eve is a nice compromise for opponents of the fireworks ban and still contributes to preserving a tradition.

120187.explanation: Not everyone is able to bring large objects to the landfill. One day per month for which residents can register would help many people.

120190.explanation: Building houses should be done systematically, with more possibilities than currently allowed. We must ensure that the open landscape of Noord-Beveland is preserved.

120192.explanation: The scale of Noord-Beveland is too small for a local broadcaster.

120197.explanation: They must act where nuisance arises.

120198.explanation: There is no need for more camera surveillance.

120203.explanation: There is a housing shortage in the Netherlands. To alleviate this, people can also live permanently in holiday homes, many of which are located in Noord-Beveland. Many of these are suitable for permanent residence.

120204.explanation: This must be done responsibly, on a small scale and based on organic growth.

120205.explanation: It is logical that a counter-performance is requested if possible. Moreover, it helps in socializing these people.

120206.explanation: It may be necessary to assist residents with a low income. This also includes workers with a low salary. Working poor often cannot make much use of benefits.

120207.explanation: The municipality does not pursue income politics. However, a small fund must be available for truly urgent cases where current regulations do not provide.

120209.explanation: There are more and more electric cars, especially with tourists visiting Noord-Beveland. Charging points must be available for this.

120210.explanation: This is already the case in many places. The short distances within the town centers mean it hardly matters in terms of time.

120211.explanation: It would be nice if such a wide range was available, but we do not have that luxury.

120215.explanation: The tourist tax has already been increased more than trend-wise in recent years. Recreation businesses also face rising costs and Noord-Beveland should not price itself out of the market. We also do not need that money.

120216.explanation: This must be done cautiously, with respect for the environment and through organic growth.

120218.explanation: (Young) house seekers who have been on a waiting list for a long time must not be passed over by refugees with a residence permit.

120223.explanation: Zeelandic is still spoken on school playgrounds, albeit to a lesser extent. The Ministry of Education and the school are responsible for the curriculum. Of course, WzNB has no objection to attention being paid to the Noord-Beveland dialect.

120226.explanation: This is an effective means to help (young) people with affordable housing.

120227.explanation: There are no parking problems in our municipality, except for some peak moments. Our roads are usually spaciously designed. WzNB supports shared cars, so that the number of cars decreases.

120228.explanation: If it can be integrated organically in a not too expensive manner, there is no objection. There is plenty of space to walk dogs.

120229.explanation: The coast belongs to all of us and should not be private property. Existing rights must not be tampered with. Building is not allowed in Natura 2000 areas in any case, leaving very little space for construction.

120230.explanation: The natural areas belong to all of us and should also be accessible to the public to enjoy nature. Exceptions may be made during the breeding season, but this must be well demonstrated.

120231.explanation: Wind turbines on private properties up to a height of 15 meters are allowed. Beyond that, the horizon of our island is already too damaged by 150 m high wind turbines and that absolutely does not fit the Noord-Beveland scale and size.

120174.explanation: Not as a strict quota; it is primarily about suitable persons. Nevertheless, a female alderman would be very fitting, alongside our female mayor.

120175.explanation: A referendum on vital issues feeds the municipal council and contributes to the trust of the population.

120178.explanation: Preferential treatment of existing farms limits young starting and innovative businesses. A campsite is allowed, provided it concerns an active farm with agricultural activity as the main source of income and no more than 25 camping units.

120179.explanation: Individual support already exists via subsidies; that is sufficient.

120180.explanation: Large distribution centers do not fit in our landscape; this leads to undesirable urbanization. There is room for small specialized distribution centers with high-quality jobs, such as ship parts and pharmaceuticals.

120182.explanation: Migrant workers / seasonal workers have the right to good housing for which the employer is responsible. Preferably on their own premises, because employers directly benefit from it. The housing must naturally meet minimum standards; otherwise accommodation must be arranged elsewhere.

120186.explanation: A fireworks show can prevent a lot of trouble, especially if the population is involved. However, the number of fireworks specialists is very limited, and they are busy in the big cities on December 31.

120187.explanation: This prevents illegal dumping and is certainly the way for less mobile residents to get rid of large bulky waste.

120190.explanation: Why would farmers be an exception? They are already favored, for example with mini campsites. Moreover, this leads to cluttering of the landscape.

120192.explanation: The municipality already does this. It is broader, about supporting local information provision. It is important that residents can access local and regional news.

120197.explanation: Our municipality is not unsafe, both objectively and in the perception of residents. But 'safety' is broader than burglaries etc.; it concerns visibility, observation ('eyes and ears of the municipality'), enforcement of standards, prevention and awareness.

120198.explanation: Cameras threaten privacy and there is currently no reason for them. Given the vastness of our municipality, problems can quickly move, making camera surveillance less effective.

120203.explanation: There are 125,000 holiday homes. Allowing habitation immediately makes extra homes available without new construction. For starters or pensioners, these are often an affordable alternative. However, many older holiday homes do not meet current insulation and building code standards.

120204.explanation: Expansion of residential areas must be possible, but adjacent to existing boundaries: a new neighborhood adjoining the village.

120205.explanation: It is good when someone gives something back to the society that supports them financially. Moreover, someone remains active and involved in society. It should only involve activities that do not displace paid work.

120206.explanation: Municipal taxes are unaffordable for many people. There is also no financial necessity to stop waiving them.

120207.explanation: The problem of incomes and benefits can only be solved nationally. If the municipality raises the threshold, it will create the same question about the threshold again.

120209.explanation: The existing charging points are largely unused outside the seasonal weeks. People with a private driveway can charge their car at home. The big problem lies in the village centers, where it is difficult if not impossible to charge the car close to home. The municipality will have to address this in due course.

120210.explanation: The centers are not designed for speed limits higher than 30 km/h; this restriction is already in effect everywhere.

120211.explanation: This no longer fits in today's society and the personnel shortages in healthcare are such that it is also not feasible.

120215.explanation: This tax is lower in Noord-Beveland than the Zeeland average. The tax must therefore be collected.

120216.explanation: It is an incentive for new companies to stay on the island or come here. This is essential for employment and in the long term for the local amenities.

120218.explanation: Status holders have an equal starting position as regular housing seekers. Since 2026, municipalities are legally prohibited from automatically giving status holders priority over other housing seekers when allocating social rental homes.

120223.explanation: That is not a task for the municipality. Moreover, 'the Zeeland dialect' does not exist. What is more important is that good education takes place in the Dutch language.

120226.explanation: There is no particular shortage of rental rooms for residence. We do not want room rentals to tourists. Homes intended for families should preferably be inhabited by families.

120227.explanation: No additional parking spaces are needed.

120228.explanation: For those centers where there is a demand for fenced areas where dogs can also run off leash. For normal dog walking, there is plenty of space everywhere.

120229.explanation: We strictly adhere to the Coastal Pact 2026-2030 to prevent further paving over of the coast. But along the banks of the Veerse Meer and the Oosterschelde, building has occurred and continues: on both sides of the harbor in Kamperland and also at the harbors of Colijnsplaat and Kats, construction is planned.

120230.explanation: The existing nature reserves are already sufficiently regulated through full or partial (breeding season) closures. However, enforcement is lacking.

120231.explanation: The existing regulation allows farmers to place a windmill up to 21 meters high. If this is relaxed, there will be a proliferation of wind turbines. This is bad for the health of residents and for the landscape. Moreover, the municipal council has already decided that no more wind turbines will be added.

statements.theme: No new construction on the coast
statements.title: No new homes may be built on the coast or along the waterfront.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The residents of Noord-Beveland must be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Noord-Beveland moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: There must be more camera surveillance in Noord-Beveland.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: More nature areas on Noord-Beveland must be closed off to the public.
Noord-Beveland must have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who deal with safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Industrial/business parks in Noord-Beveland may be allowed to expand.
The municipality must make it easier for farmers to place wind turbines on their own fields and farmyards.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het voor boeren makkelijker maken om

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

titleParts.text: te plaatsen op hun eigen akkers en erven.

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include attention to the Zeeuws dialect.
There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: Villages and small towns that are part of the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: There must be a dog-walking/exercise area in every village.

titleParts.glossary: A village or small town that is part of the municipality.
